theme: Cutting a stiff sheet until it bends like leather
title: Living-Hinge Curvature Fit
seed_problem: novel composite family (wave2b-lens:geometry-fabrication); no classic decision-problem ancestor
mutation: composite: cubic compliance-to-curvature inverse design (compliance-to-curvature-mapping) + a hard per-column net-section-strength-floor + a single shared cross-column slit budget that turns per-column inverse-cubic fitting into a multiple-choice knapsack (slit-pattern-synthesis)
strategies: do-nothing baseline: slit nothing, every column stays at full material (checker's own baseline construction)

tangent-line monotone greedy: linearize the cubic compliance law at full material, estimate cuts needed per column from that linear model, and serve the neediest-looking columns first until the shared budget runs out
exact-inverse multiple-choice knapsack: compute the true cubic cost/error curve per column and solve the shared-budget allocation exactly with a DP over the budget dimension

uniform slit density (trap pattern from the classic living-hinge folklore): spend the budget evenly across all columns, which can only realize a nearly constant curvature and badly misses any varying target profile
